rationale_for_baseline_tier: A 4-month-old, pre-verbal infant with 6 hours of acute severe pain on hip movement, new apparent limb shortening and reduced spontaneous movement of the leg. The likely diagnosis (an acutely unstable/dislocated dysplastic hip) and the key can't-miss mimic (septic arthritis of the hip) both require hands-on examination and imaging that telehealth cannot provide, and septic arthritis cannot be excluded remotely in a non-verbal infant despite a normal home temperature. This warrants urgent SAME-DAY in-person assessment (T3 — urgent same-day / urgent care, via urgent paediatric review or a paediatric emergency department). It is not a stable chronic problem (so not T1/T2 routine referral), and — while currently afebrile and well-perfused — it is not yet a 000 ambulance emergency (T5); the deterioration triggers escalate it there. Under-triage (routine referral / reassurance) is the dangerous error here.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Develops a fever (>=38 C), becomes systemically unwell, or refuses all feeds
escalation_edges.rationale: Fever or feed refusal in this setting raises septic arthritis/sepsis — a time-critical emergency needing immediate assessment and IV treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she develops a fever, becomes floppy or very unwell, or refuses all her feeds, call 000 or go straight to the emergency department — do not wait for the booked appointment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The leg becomes pale, cold, blue or mottled
escalation_edges.rationale: A pale/cold leg indicates compromised circulation — a limb-threatening emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the leg turns pale, cold, blue or blotchy, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline: acutely painful, poorly-moving infant hip with new apparent shortening, afebrile and well-perfused
escalation_edges.rationale: Needs urgent in-person hip examination, exclusion of septic arthritis and dynamic ultrasound today; telehealth cannot examine or image the hip.
escalation_edges.communication_template: She needs to be seen and examined in person today — please attend an urgent paediatric review or your nearest emergency department this afternoon for an examination and an ultrasound of the hip.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Pain becomes uncontrollable despite the analgesia the carer already has
escalation_edges.rationale: Escalating, uncontrolled pain in an infant warrants emergency department assessment rather than waiting for an outpatient slot.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If her pain is not settling at all and she cannot be comforted, take her to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: 4-month-old, pre-verbal infant who cannot describe or localise symptoms
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for in-person review — the infant cannot self-report, so history is limited and hands-on examination is essential. No paediatric dose may be synthesised; dosing is deferred to in-person clinician assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Extremely anxious first-time mother; grandmother present and supportive
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, calm, specific instructions and confirm the carer knows exactly where to take the baby today and which signs mean call 000; use the second carer to help with safe handling and transport.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your baby needs to be examined in person today. Please attend an urgent paediatric review or your nearest emergency department this afternoon for a hip examination and an ultrasound scan — a video call cannot do these. While you get ready, keep her hips gently bent up and apart (frog-leg), carry her straddled on your hip, avoid forcing the legs together or straight, and roll her side to side for nappy changes. You can continue the infant paracetamol you have already been giving, but do not start any new medicine dose without a clinician.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend urgent in-person paediatric assessment / paediatric emergency department today; call 000 if any emergency sign appears
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If this is after hours, go to the nearest emergency department rather than waiting for a GP or clinic to open — this needs same-day assessment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth encounter: 4-month-old, ~6 h acute pain on left hip abduction, new apparent left leg shortening, reduced left-leg movement; afebrile on home thermometer, limb well-perfused; risk factors female + family history + asymmetric creases. Unable to examine or image via telehealth; escalated for urgent same-day in-person assessment to exclude septic arthritis and arrange dynamic hip ultrasound and paediatric orthopaedic review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring the family or arranging only a routine/prompt GP or weeks-away referral, treating this as benign DDH or teething and missing the unexcluded septic joint.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A missed septic arthritis of the hip destroys the joint within hours and can progress to sepsis; delayed reduction/bracing of an unstable hip risks avascular necrosis and permanent disability.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Booking an in-person review but not same-day, allowing an acutely painful, non-moving infant limb to wait.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance for a currently afebrile, well-perfused, feeding infant when urgent same-day in-person assessment (or self-transport to ED) is appropriate — acceptable caution, but not the baseline need.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot distinguish an acutely unstable dysplastic hip from septic arthritis (or occult fracture) in a pre-verbal infant, and cannot exclude a septic joint on a normal home temperature.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the uncertainty honestly, do not commit to a benign diagnosis remotely, and arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment with examination and imaging that can resolve it.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using reassuring negatives (no fever, no trauma, family history of DDH) to settle on 'just DDH' and a routine referral, or attempting remote examination/reduction.
